name: Savoy cabbage (food medium)

components.name: D-glucose
components.source: USDA FDC 170388 (total sugars 2.27 g) apportioned by USDA FDC 169975 'Cabbage, raw' free-sugar profile (glu 1.67/fru 1.45/suc 0.08 of 3.20 g)
components.evidence: Glucose is the leading free sugar in head cabbage; scaled to savoy total sugars (2.27 g). Co-dominant fermentable carbon source.

components.name: D-fructose
components.source: USDA FDC 170388 total sugars apportioned by USDA FDC 169975 free-sugar ratio
components.evidence: Second most abundant free sugar in cabbage; near-equimolar with glucose. Co-dominant fermentable carbon source.

components.name: sucrose
components.source: USDA FDC 170388 total sugars apportioned by USDA FDC 169975 (sucrose 0.08/3.20 g)
components.evidence: Minor disaccharide in cabbage; <3% of total sugars.

components.name: maltose
components.source: USDA FDC 170388 total sugars apportioned by USDA FDC 169975 (maltose 0.01/3.20 g)

components.name: L-malic acid
components.source: Cabbage juice HPLC literature ~6 mM malate (Fleming/sauerkraut studies) => ~0.08 g/100g FW; malic identified/quantified in Brassica oleracea leaves by Sousa & Valentao (doi:10.1007/s00217-005-0104-0)
components.evidence: Malic acid is one of the two dominant organic acids of Brassica oleracea; primary organic-acid carbon/energy source. Amount is a fresh-weight estimate from cabbage-juice molarity, not a savoy-specific assay.

components.name: citric acid
components.source: Sousa, Valentao et al., Brassica oleracea organic-acid HPLC-UV (doi:10.1007/s00217-005-0104-0; PMID 16277412)
components.evidence: Citric acid is co-dominant with malic in Brassica oleracea leaves (predominant in inner leaves). Present but not quantified for savoy specifically; growth-relevant organic acid. NOT in the original FooDB profile.

components.name: oxalic acid
components.source: Oxalate food-composition surveys: cabbage classified low-oxalate <10 mg/100g FW (Guinea Lynx / oxalatecontent.com; retention study doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2011.09.005)
components.evidence: Low but present; ~4-10 mg/100g FW. Dehydrated cabbage powder ~49 mg/100g (PMC9737565) confirms low native level.

components.name: fumaric acid
components.source: Sousa & Valentao, Brassica oleracea organic acids (doi:10.1007/s00217-005-0104-0)
components.evidence: Minor TCA-cycle organic acid consistently detected in Brassica oleracea leaves; present, not quantified for savoy.

components.name: succinic acid
components.evidence: TCA-cycle acid present at minor levels; unquantified. Growth-relevant if present.

components.name: quinic acid
components.source: Sousa/Valentao Brassica oleracea var. acephala/costata organic acids (PMID 19722523)
components.evidence: Detected in Brassica oleracea tissues; present, not quantified.

components.name: shikimic acid
components.evidence: Detected in Brassica oleracea leaves; present, unquantified.

components.name: aconitic acid
components.evidence: TCA-associated acid identified in Brassica oleracea leaves; present, unquantified.

components.name: total protein
components.evidence: Bulk N/amino-acid source; individual amino acids below are the protein-derived profile.

components.name: L-glutamate
components.evidence: Most abundant amino acid; includes glutamine.

components.name: L-serine
components.evidence: Protein-derived L-serine (replaces the D-serine mis-annotation in the FooDB profile).

components.name: vitamin C (L-ascorbate)
components.source: USDA FDC 170388 (savoy 31 mg; savoy-specific study reports ~49 mg, doi:10.17660/ActaHortic.2012.939.38)
components.evidence: Also an organic acid/antioxidant; growth-relevant micronutrient.

components.name: biotin (B7)
components.source: not in USDA FDC 170388; present in Brassica vegetables at low ug/100g levels
components.evidence: Growth-relevant cofactor; present but unquantified for savoy.

components.name: vitamin A (RAE)
components.evidence: Provitamin-A activity from carotenoids (no preformed retinol in this plant).

components.name: beta-carotene
components.source: USDA FDC 170388 (savoy-specific ~370 ug reported by doi:10.17660/ActaHortic.2012.939.38)

components.name: lutein
components.evidence: Xanthophyll carotenoid; no BiGG met but genuine constituent.

components.name: chloride
components.source: FooDB profile; genuine anion in cabbage tissue

components.name: sulfate
components.source: FooDB profile; sulfur source (also glucosinolate-bound S)

components.name: nickel
components.source: FooDB profile; trace micronutrient in plants
components.evidence: Trace cofactor (urease/hydrogenase); low but genuine; retained.

components.name: linoleic acid (18:2 n-6)
components.evidence: Dominant PUFA of the (very low) lipid fraction.

components.name: alpha-linolenic acid (18:3 n-3)
components.evidence: Major PUFA in cabbage leaf lipids.

components.name: dietary fiber
components.evidence: Largely non-fermentable by simple GEMs; context only.

remove.name: Retinol
remove.reason: Preformed retinol (animal-form vitamin A) does not occur in a plant food; savoy provides provitamin A as beta-carotene. FooDB mis-assignment.

remove.name: (R)-Oxypeucedanin
remove.reason: Furanocoumarin characteristic of Apiaceae/Rutaceae, not Brassicaceae; biologically implausible in cabbage. FooDB predicted/misassigned noise.

remove.name: epsilon-Polylysine
remove.reason: Microbially produced food preservative (Streptomyces), not a native cabbage metabolite. FooDB noise.

remove.name: (±)-erythro-Isoleucine
remove.reason: Stereochemical artifact/duplicate of L-isoleucine; not a distinct nutrient.

remove.name: (E)-2-Phenyl-2-butenal
remove.reason: Trace flavor aldehyde, not a growth nutrient; negligible and non-fermentable.

remove.name: D-Serine
remove.reason: Plant protein contains L-serine, not the D-enantiomer; replaced with L-serine (USDA value). FooDB stereochemical mis-annotation.

missing_added: D-glucose, D-fructose, sucrose, maltose (specific fermentable sugars with amounts; FooDB profile had none)
L-malic acid (co-dominant organic acid; primary organic-acid carbon source)
citric acid (co-dominant organic acid; was entirely absent)
oxalic, fumaric, succinic, quinic, shikimic, aconitic acids
L-phenylalanine and L-valine (amino acids missing from FooDB profile)
vitamin K, vitamin E, biotin (B7), lutein, palmitic/stearic acid, dietary fiber, selenium
keep_uncovered: myricetin (genuine cabbage flavonoids, no BiGG met)
glucosinolates (sinigrin, glucobrassicin - defining Brassica phytochemicals, no BiGG met)
sources: USDA FoodData Central FDC 170388 (Cabbage, savoy, raw) - full nutrient panel
USDA FoodData Central FDC 169975 (Cabbage, raw) - free-sugar ratio used to apportion savoy total sugars
Sousa, Valentao, Andrade et al., Brassica oleracea organic acids by HPLC-UV (Eur Food Res Technol doi:10.1007/s00217-005-0104-0; PMID 16277412; PMID 19722523)
Sauerkraut/cabbage-juice HPLC literature (~6 mM malate) for malic-acid FW estimate
Oxalate composition surveys (cabbage low-oxalate <10 mg/100g FW; retention study doi:10.1016/j.jfca.2011.09.005; cabbage powder PMC9737565)
Savoy-cabbage phytochemical study (ActaHortic doi:10.17660/ActaHortic.2012.939.38): vitamin C ~49, beta-carotene ~0.37, lutein ~0.18 mg/100g
FooDB FOOD00002 (source of original component list)
notes: Dominant carbon/energy sources for a microbe growing on savoy cabbage are the near-equimolar free hexoses (glucose ~1.2 + fructose ~1.0 g/100g) plus the organic acids malic and citric (co-dominant in Brassica oleracea). The original FooDB profile listed zero free sugars and zero organic acids - the largest growth-relevant gap - and mis-included retinol, oxypeucedanin, polylysine and D-serine. USDA amino-acid/mineral/vitamin/fatty-acid values are high-confidence; organic-acid amounts are literature-anchored estimates (malic, oxalic) or present-but-unquantified (citric et al.), hence medium overall confidence. Generic model-medium species (H2O, CO2, O2, H+, N2, NH4+) from the exchange set are not food-composition nutrients and were left to the medium template.
